name: How to Properly Report a Neck Injury at Work Without Getting Denied
description: How to Properly Report a Neck Injury at Work Without Getting Denied - Salt Lake City

step.name: Step 1
step.text: Reporting a neck injury is the most critical step in the workers\' compensation process. Because neck injuries often manifest as stiffness or "whiplash" that people try to ignore, many workers wait too long to speak up. This guide provides the exact steps you should take to protect your rights in Utah and Idaho.

step.name: Step 2
step.text: ### Step 1: Notify Your Supervisor in Writing

step.name: Step 3
step.text: Verbal notice is not enough. Send an email or text message so you have a time-stamped record. State exactly when the pain started and what you were doing. For example: "While I was reaching overhead to stock the top shelf at 10:00 AM today, I felt a sharp pinch in my neck."

step.name: Step 4
step.text: ### Step 2: Request an Authorized Medical Provider

step.name: Step 5
step.text: Under Utah law, the employer usually has the right to choose the doctor for the first visit. Ask your manager, "Which clinic should I go to for a work-related injury?" Going to your own doctor first can lead to the insurance company refusing to pay the bill.

step.name: Step 7
step.text: When you see the doctor, tell them about every symptom. Neck injuries often cause pain, numbness, or tingling that travels down the arms and into the hands. This is called radiculopathy. It is a sign of a more serious nerve issue or disc herniation. If you don't tell the doctor about your arm symptoms, they may only diagnose you with a minor strain, which lowers the value of your claim.

step.name: Step 9
step.text: If the doctor gives you a "Work Status" note that says "No lifting over 5 pounds," you must follow it—both at work and at home. If the insurance company hires a private investigator and catches you carrying groceries that look heavier than 5 pounds, they will use that video to deny your entire claim for fraud.

step.name: Step 11
step.text: Maintain a folder with every piece of paper you receive. This includes:

step.name: Step 15
step.text: *   A log of every person you speak to at the insurance company.

step.name: Step 16
step.text: If your claim is delayed for more than 21 days, or if you are told your neck pain is just "age-related," contact the attorneys at Just Work Comp Law at 801-746-0290. We can help you file the necessary paperwork to get your medical treatment approved.
